metadata.industry: Charter transportation marketplace (buses, vans, mini-buses) for Brazilian tourism
metadata.positioning: The Trivago of charter transportation — clean, trustworthy, technology-forward, optimistic

navy.usage: primary brand, headlines, primary buttons, footers, navbar text

green.usage: accent, CTAs, focus rings, journey highlights, semantic keyword in bicolor titles

colors.ctaContrastRule: For green CTAs with text < 18px, prefer navy text on green bg (or use green-700 instead of green-500)

heading.fallbackQuality: Poppins is acceptable substitute if Gotham fails to load

rules.uppercase: AVOID in long paragraphs (clarity over stylization)
rules.alignment: left default; centered only for CTAs and short hero titles

bicolorTitlePattern.name: bv-heading-bicolor
bicolorTitlePattern.description: ONE word in green (semantic keyword), rest in navy. Brand signature visual.
bicolorTitlePattern.rule: Never split colors within a single word
bicolorTitlePattern.selectionHeuristic: The green word should be the semantic keyword — the action, destination, or emotional pivot. Navy holds the structural/preposition parts.

5.use: gap between blocks of same section

layout.responsivePhilosophy: Design desktop and mobile separately — not just 'responsive'. Sidebar on desktop becomes bottom-sheet on mobile. Hero stacks vertically. CTAs become floating action buttons on long pages.

motion.reducedMotionFallback: All shimmer skeletons become pulse 1s ease-in-out infinite. Page transitions become instant. Carousel autoplay disabled.

philosophy.name: Blueprint Planner
philosophy.shortDescription: The clarity of a technical document meeting the warmth of a journey
philosophy.longDescription: Buscou Viajou's visual language is grounded in the geometric clarity of an architectural blueprint — clean lines, structured grids, navy ink — but warmed by green accents that suggest movement and growth. It speaks the language of an organized planner who happens to be excited about your next trip.
antiReferences: Ornate or elaborate (e.g. travel agency vintage)
Skeuomorphic (e.g. tickets resembling paper boarding passes)
Generic AI illustration with no brand voice

values.name: Simplicidade
values.description: Acreditamos que viajar deve ser fácil. Soluções simples e intuitivas pra encontrar, comparar e reservar sem complicação.
values.visualExpression: outline cards (white bg, 2px green border, navy text)
values.voiceExpression: short sentences, journey verbs, no jargon

values.name: Inovação
values.description: Buscamos novas tecnologias e formas de melhorar a experiência. Busca e contratação cada vez mais rápida e inteligente.
values.visualExpression: accent card (green bg, white text, optional rotate -4deg in marketing)

values.name: Conexão
values.description: Conectamos pessoas a destinos, experiências e oportunidades. Rede de mobilidade acessível e eficiente.
values.visualExpression: brand card (navy bg, white text, solid block)
values.voiceExpression: inclusive, second-person, talking with not at

personality.tone: próxima e otimista, não corporativa, não excessivamente informal

principles: CTAs as journey verbs (Buscar, Encontrar, Comparar, Reservar, Viajar)
Avoid technical jargon (no 'onboarding', 'conversion rate', 'CTA', 'LTV' in user-facing text)
Show, don't promise. Be specific over generic.

good: Não encontramos veículos pra essa rota.
avoid: Insira o destino de partida no campo abaixo
Nenhum resultado encontrado para os critérios fornecidos
Sua transação foi processada com sucesso

imagery.subjects: Real people in mobility context — drivers smiling, passengers embarking, professionals in uniform, travelers with luggage

vehicles.graphic: green wave/curve graphic on side panel + BV monogram

imagery.framing: people with eye contact or positive gestures (wave, thumbs up)
doNotUse: Stock photos with exaggerated retouching or unrealistic smiles
Dark night scenes (conflicts with trust/clarity territory)
Tense or chaotic transport scenes (rush, traffic jams shown negatively)
Generic transportation stock without brand context
Maps or routes drawn over photos (use SVG overlays only)

layoutPrinciples.whitespace: generous; respiro is part of the brand identity

asymmetry.marketing: OK — rotated cards, off-center heroes, dynamic compositions
asymmetry.transactional: AVOID — forms, dashboards, reservation flow stay aligned and predictable

informationDensity.search results: medium — comparison needs density but not crowding
informationDensity.details: medium — generous but no scrolling fatigue
informationDensity.dashboard: high — but with clear visual hierarchy

iconography.style: outline, 2px stroke, rounded caps and joins

blueprintGrid.name: bv-canvas
blueprintGrid.description: Blueprint paper aesthetic. 32×32px grid of subtle lines on light background. Reinforces 'planner' territory.

css.background-image: linear-gradient(to right, var(--bv-color-grid-line) 1px, transparent 1px), linear-gradient(to bottom, var(--bv-color-grid-line) 1px, transparent 1px)

frameCorner.name: bv-frame-corner
frameCorner.description: L-shaped brackets in navy 2px stroke at top-left and bottom-right of an element. Suggests hand-drawn route corners around hero images.

bicolorTitle.name: bv-heading-bicolor
bicolorTitle.description: Brand signature: one word in green (the semantic keyword), rest in navy. THIS IS THE MOST IMPORTANT VISUAL PATTERN OF THE BRAND.
rules: ONE word in green, rest in navy
Never split colors within the same word
Green word should be the action, destination, or emotional pivot

journeyTag.name: bv-journey-tag
journeyTag.description: 'Buscou → Encontrou → Viajou' as a closing signature — the brand's hero tagline reduced to its three movements.
journeyTag.useSparingly: Don't repeat in same view — it's a signature, not a pattern

valueCardTriad.description: Three card treatments forming the Values section pattern (page 3 of brand manual).
valueCardTriad.rule: Order: Outline → Accent (rotated) → Brand. The rotation creates rhythm; the colors create hierarchy.

buttonRing.description: Focus ring is ALWAYS green — never grey or blue — for accessibility AND brand consistency.
buttonRing.appliesTo: all interactive elements (buttons, links, inputs, cards) on focus-visible

greenWave.description: Curved green line on side of vehicles in brand imagery — derived from BV monogram dynamic stroke.
greenWave.demoStatus: deferred — not implemented in MVP. Plan for Fase 2.

skeletonPattern.description: Loading state — structural skeletons that match the final layout. NEVER generic spinners.
skeletonPattern.shimmerColor: linear-gradient with white/40 moving left-to-right over 1.5s
skeletonPattern.block: use --bv-color-bg as base, slightly tinted shimmer

V2_white_on_navy.use: navy hero blocks, footers, navy marketing

states.loading: spinner replaces icon, label changes to gerund (Buscando..., Reservando...)

accent.use: marketing, optionally rotated -2 to -4deg in heroes

rules: Labels NEVER contain 'Insira', 'Digite', 'Informe' — use direct question
Placeholders use real-world examples, not field descriptions
Error messages start with what's wrong, then how to fix

mobile.drawer: right side sheet, full height, navy bg with white links

skeleton.rule: Skeleton blocks must match the layout dimensions of final content (use same width/height as actual elements)

toast.errorIdRule: 5xx errors include error_id in toast body (small caption text)

preferredPhrasings.emptyResults: Não encontramos veículos pra essa rota e data.

punctuation.endingPeriods: Optional in single-line headlines, required in paragraphs

examples: CPF inválido. Verifique os números digitados.
Data muito próxima. A viagem precisa ser pelo menos amanhã.
Cidade não encontrada. Tente São Paulo, Rio, BH ou outra capital.

doNotList: Don't use grey neutrals in shadows — always navy-tinted
Don't mix more than 2 colors in a single title (navy + green only)
Don't use uppercase for body paragraphs
Don't put green on text smaller than 18px (or 14px bold) without testing contrast
Don't render bare placeholder spinners — use structural skeletons
Don't use rotated cards in transactional product UI (forms, dashboards) — only marketing
Don't write 'Click here' or 'Insira seu...' — use journey verbs and direct labels
Don't use the descriptor 'Fretamento Inteligente' isolated — always with logo
Don't apply 3D effects, gradients, drop shadows, or bevels to the logo
Don't use grey hover states on logos — use color variants or opacity
Don't use stock photos with exaggerated retouching
Don't put the journey tag (Buscou → Encontrou → Viajou) more than once per view
Don't use blueprint grid in dashboards or tables — visual fatigue
Don't render frame-corner brackets around small elements — minimum 200px on each side
Don't break the bicolor pattern by splitting colors within a single word
Don't use spinner-only loading — always pair with structural skeleton or label
Don't auto-play carousels without user-pause control
Don't use modal for non-blocking confirmations — prefer toast with undo when possible

rules: All interactive elements must have visible focus state (green ring shadow-focus)
Color is never the sole indicator (always pair with icon or label)
Modals trap focus and restore on close
Skip-link to main content on every page
All images with semantic value have alt text in pt-BR
Buttons describe their action ('Solicitar reserva', not 'OK')
Heading hierarchy never skipped (h1 → h2 → h3)
Reduced motion respected via prefers-reduced-motion media query
accessibility.testing: axe-core scan in @a11y E2E suite, zero violations of severity 'serious' or higher
